---
title: "Clark County, Washington"
subtitle: "2019-2021"
number: 7
image: /images/clark_WA.jpg
image-alt: "Image of Clark County, Washington"
categories: ["MRF"]
---
{{< include _return-to-pf.qmd >}}

::: {.article}

# Transfer System and Recycling Processing Cost of Service Analysis

Bell & Associates assisted Clark County with calculating to cost of service for
the County’s three transfer stations and regional material recovery facility as
part of a long-term system study. The transfer system is operated by Columbia
Resource Company (Waste Connections) under the regulatory oversight of the
County. The accounting for three transfer stations and one MRF was under one
profit center, which significantly increased the complexity of the project. In
2019, the system disposed of over 435,000 tons of solid waste and processed
over 60,000 tons of recyclable materials.

Reference: Mike Davis

# 2021 MRF Cost of Service Study

# 2015 Material Recovery Facility Revenue Analysis

[By Visitor7 - Own work, CC BY-SA 3.0](https://commons.wikimedia.org/w/index.php?curid=29713329)
:::
